description: Move slash command discovery and results into Compact Terminal flow: compact palette near input, command outputs as chronological transcript rows, and picker/menu commands as terminal-native overlays.

userStories.title: Render compact slash palette anchored to input
userStories.description: As a keyboard user, I want typing slash commands to show a dense local palette without losing transcript context.
acceptanceCriteria: Typing / shows a compact palette visually anchored to the prompt/input area, not an indistinct top-of-screen list
Palette entries use the shared slash command catalog for /status, /session, /thinking, /model, /login, aliases, and CLI navigation commands where available
Selected row has a clear marker such as ❯; command names use action/highlight tone; descriptions and unsupported reasons are dim/secondary
Filtering /st preserves Web command names and aliases and only shows matching commands or compact unavailable/deferred groups
Enter on an exact command runs it; Enter on a prefix accepts the command and leaves a trailing space for arguments
Escape closes the palette without deleting typed input on first Escape; Up/Down/Enter behavior is tested
Focused controller and Ink render tests pass
PTY artifact shows / and /st with input plus palette as one visual unit
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Passed 2026-05-17. Implemented input-anchored slash palette by rendering suggestions after the transcript and immediately before the prompt. Verified shared catalog/filtering (/status, /session, /thinking, /model, /login, aliases and unavailable items), selection marker, Escape preservation, Enter prefix acceptance, and Up/Down reducer behavior with focused tests. PTY artifact (mocked default TUI path): /workspace/.tmp/ink-cli-terminal-rendering-parity/prd03-2026-05-17/slash-suggestions-status-thinking with raw.ansi.log, clean.txt, screen.txt, metadata.json, input.json, assertions.json, events.jsonl. Commands: npm run build; node --test test/ink-cli-v2-pty-smoke.test.mjs test/cli-ui-session-app.test.mjs; npm run typecheck; npm test; node scripts/ink-cli-v2-pty-smoke.mjs --scenario slash-suggestions-status-thinking --artifact-root .tmp/ink-cli-terminal-rendering-parity/prd03-2026-05-17.

userStories.title: Normalize command results into transcript rows
userStories.description: As a TUI user, I want executed command output to appear where it happened in the terminal history.
acceptanceCriteria: Add or refine a renderer-neutral or CLI-local converter from CommandResultDescriptor to CompactTerminalRow[] plus TerminalCardDescriptor-compatible data
/status appends an execution.command row and a tool.status row/card and does not put the status payload in state.message
Unsupported commands and command errors render as terminal error/unsupported rows with redacted secret values, not only header errors
Pure guidance remains allowed in state.message only for picker instructions, suggestion hints, cancellations, and non-history empty states
Converted local rows use stable ids/source ids to support later dedupe if runtime events echo them
Focused converter/controller tests pass
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Passed 2026-05-17. Added/used CLI-local CommandResultDescriptor -> CompactTerminalRow[] normalization with stable local ids and redacted descriptor text. /status now appends execution.command plus tool.status rows and clears picker/suggestions without placing status payload in state.message. Unsupported commands and errors append terminal rows. Focused tests: status command result rows preserve transcript flow; /status closes an open picker; /status preserves streaming rows; command errors append redacted transcript rows. Commands: npm run build; node --test test/cli-ui-session-app.test.mjs test/cli-ui-ink-renderer.test.mjs test/terminal-parity-fixtures.test.mjs test/session-ui-view-models.test.mjs; npm run typecheck; npm test. Evidence tier: source/unit plus mocked PTY artifacts listed in US-001/US-004.

userStories.title: Apply transcript flow to all command result families
userStories.description: As a Web Chat user, I want TUI commands to preserve the same visible action semantics as Web.
acceptanceCriteria: /thinking <level>, /model <provider/model>, /login <provider/method>, /fast, /compact, /clone, /abort, /kill, and /kill-all produce rows/cards or picker overlays according to their shared result type
Menu-opening commands use overlays for selection but any completed action/result is appended as a chronological terminal row
If /clone opens or switches to a derived session, the originating command result remains visible before or during the transition and is not silently lost
Errors from model/login/thinking/clone/abort/kill flows redact secrets and preserve high-signal error markers
Focused command-family tests cover success, unsupported, picker, session-link, and error descriptors
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Passed 2026-05-17. Applied transcript-row flow to direct /thinking, /model, /login, /fast, /compact, /clone, /abort, /kill, /kill-all, /fork-candidates, /session-current, /sessions, terminal download/upload, unsupported, session-link, and error descriptors. Menu-opening commands still use keyboard overlays; completed menu selections append command/result rows. Clone/fork session-opening results carry local command rows into the opened session transcript. Focused tests cover success, unsupported, picker, session-link/open-session, and redacted error descriptors. PTY slash scenario covers /download unsupported row and /thinking menu selection. Evidence tier: source/unit plus mocked PTY.

userStories.title: Preserve ordering across pickers and streaming state
userStories.description: As an operator, I want commands run during overlays or streaming to keep correct transcript order.
acceptanceCriteria: /status while owner/room/session picker is open closes or suspends the picker and appends command/status rows after existing transcript rows
/status while an assistant/tool row is streaming preserves running rows and appends command/status rows after the current visible transcript tail
Existing transcript rows remain unchanged when command rows are appended
Picker state cleanup is explicit and tested; no command output remains only in header/message state
Focused controller tests cover picker-open, streaming, empty-session, and existing-transcript cases
PTY artifact captures /status while a picker is open and shows status in transcript flow, not as a header message
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Passed 2026-05-17. Command execution over an open picker closes/suspends picker state and appends rows after existing transcript rows. Running assistant/tool rows remain unchanged and /status appends after the visible transcript tail. state.message remains guidance-only for picker instructions, slash acceptance, cancellation, and empty states. PTY artifact for /status while room picker is open: /workspace/.tmp/ink-cli-terminal-rendering-parity/prd03-2026-05-17/status-while-picker. Focused tests cover picker-open, streaming, and existing-transcript ordering. Evidence tier: source/unit plus mocked default TUI PTY.

userStories.title: Validate slash command flow through PTY and final command gates
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want screen evidence for slash palette and command result behavior before marking the flow complete.
acceptanceCriteria: PTY scenarios cover typing /, filtering /st, running /status, unsupported command handling, and one menu command such as /thinking, /model, or /login where feasible
Artifacts include raw ANSI, clean text, final screen, metadata, input/assertions, and exact command invocations
Clean/screen output proves command row and result row appear immediately above the input/current prompt after execution
Evidence notes classify each PTY path as mocked/fake/demo or real/default and explain any deferred live-provider limitation
Focused PTY tests pass
Full npm test passes before this PRD is marked complete
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Passed 2026-05-17. PTY scenarios cover typing /, filtering /st, accepting/running /status, unsupported /download handling, and /thinking menu flow. Artifacts include raw ANSI, clean text, final screen, metadata, input/assertions, and events; visual conversion fallback remains documented in the existing audit report. Clean/screen output shows command/result rows above the prompt. Evidence classification: mocked default TUI path, no live provider. Commands: node scripts/ink-cli-v2-pty-smoke.mjs --scenario slash-suggestions-status-thinking --artifact-root .tmp/ink-cli-terminal-rendering-parity/prd03-2026-05-17; node dist/bin/pibo.js debug pty run ... status-while-picker; npm run typecheck; npm test (669 tests).
